task_completion.description: Agent successfully logs into the OpenEMR system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenEMR dashboard, calendar, or main menu is visible after the login screen.

task_completion.description: Agent searches for the specific patient using the Patient Finder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Patient Finder search bar contains 'Maria' or 'Hickle', and search results showing 'Maria Hickle' are visible.

task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the dashboard or demographics summary of the correct patient.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The patient context header or summary screen displays 'Maria Hickle' and her DOB '1965-12-02'.

task_completion.description: Agent enters the editing mode for patient demographics.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Edit Demographics' screen or a form with editable fields for Name, DOB, and Status is visible.

task_completion.description: Agent unchecks the 'Patient Active' box or changes the status to Inactive.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Patient Active' checkbox is clearly visible and is unchecked, or a status dropdown is set to 'Inactive'.

task_completion.description: Agent saves the changes and the patient is confirmed as inactive in the system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message is displayed, or the patient's name in the header appears in red (indicating inactive status), or the read-only demographics view shows the 'Patient Active' field is empty/unchecked.

integrity.description: Agent interacts with the OpenEMR web interface via a browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser window with the OpenEMR interface, not a terminal or database console.

integrity.description: Agent does not use browser developer tools or console scripts to force the state change.
integrity.visual_evidence: No developer tools (Inspect Element, Console) are open or being typed into.

integrity.description: The interface shown is the actual OpenEMR application, not a text editor or mock-up.
integrity.visual_evidence: Standard OpenEMR UI elements (sidebar, frames, colors) are present.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient Name: Maria Hickle. DOB: 1965-12-02. The goal is to change the patient's status from Active to Inactive. In OpenEMR, this is typically done by unchecking a 'Patient Active' checkbox in the Demographics Edit screen. When inactive, the patient's name often turns red in the header.
